commit.message:
fix: use systemMessage append for agent identity, not customAgents

The cron extension's working pattern: load SOUL.md + agent files,
concatenate them, pass as systemMessage { mode: 'append', content }.
This appends the identity to the SDK's built-in system prompt.

The customAgents approach requires agent selection and is designed
for sub-agent invocation, not primary identity injection.

commit.message:
feat: support go install and fix devcontainer (#39)

* feat: support go install by moving CLI entrypoint to repo root

Move package main from cmd/ to the repository root so that
`go install github.com/manisbindra/sbi@latest` produces a binary
named `sbi` instead of the unusable `cmd`.

- Move all cmd/*.go files to repo root, remove cmd/ directory
- Rename CLI identity from daily-recommendations to sbi
- Update Taskfile.yml: project name and build path
- Update .goreleaser.yml: project name, IDs, remove dir: cmd
- Update CI workflows: binary paths in nightly-scan and scan-smoke-test
- Update README.md: add go install section, update CLI examples
- Update AGENTS.md: binary name, examples, project structure

Closes #38

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add sudo to devcontainer install-tools.sh for /usr/local/bin writes

onCreateCommand runs as the vscode user which lacks write permissions
to /usr/local/bin. Add sudo to the syft, trivy, and golangci-lint
install commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update devcontainer Go image from 1.24 to 1.26

go.mod requires go >= 1.26.1 but the devcontainer image was pinned to
Go 1.24, causing build and test failures inside the container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update go.mod to Go 1.26.2 to resolve stdlib vulnerabilities

Go 1.26.1 has known vulnerabilities in crypto/x509, crypto/tls,
archive/tar, and html/template (GO-2026-4864 through GO-2026-4947),
all fixed in Go 1.26.2. govulncheck now passes cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Update reset-database.yml binary path from daily-recommendations to sbi
- Fix goreleaser archive: repositories.txt → repositories.json
- README: restructure Quick Start to use go install, add Build from Source
- AGENTS.md: add go install command to CLI Usage section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility (#1315)

* Remove fastText support and enable numpy 2.x compatibility

FastText library has been archived since March 2024 and is no longer
maintained. This dependency was blocking numpy 2.x adoption.

Changes:
- Remove fasttext extra from setup.py dependencies
- Add deprecation warnings to fasttext.py module
- Update documentation to recommend langdetect as primary option
- Update CLI to deprecate --fasttext flag
- Add comprehensive test suite for langdetect integration
- Remove fasttext from tox.ini test configurations

Users should migrate to langdetect:
  pip install dateparser[langdetect]

Closes #1287
Closes #1251

* Update dateparser_cli/cli.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update dateparser/custom_language_detection/fasttext.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify fasttext deprecation to always error

The previous approach was confusing:
- If fasttext was installed, it showed a warning but still worked
- If fasttext wasn't installed, it raised an ImportError

This creates inconsistent deprecation - users don't know if the
feature is truly deprecated or not.

Now the module:
- Always shows deprecation warning on import
- Always raises ImportError when detect_languages() is called
- Provides clear migration path to langdetect
- No functional fasttext code remains

This makes the deprecation clear and consistent.

* Remove unused module-level mock in test_language_detect

The module-level 'detect_languages' mock was defined but never used.
The MockLangDetectTest class defines its own mock as a class attribute,
and other test classes use the actual lang_detect_detect_languages function.

This cleanup removes the unused mock to avoid confusion.

commit.message:
fix: write credentials as UTF-8 blobs via Win32 CredWrite

cmdkey stores credential blobs as UTF-16LE. The copilot CLI reads them
via keytar, which interprets blobs as UTF-8. The result: every other
byte is 0x00, Headers.append rejects the token as invalid, and auth
silently fails.

Replace cmdkey with direct Win32 CredWriteW calls that encode the
token as UTF-8 bytes — matching exactly what keytar expects.

commit.message:
perf: pool value vectors, flatten child_vals, persist scratch across iterations

Reduce per-node allocation churn in the range-vector CFR traversal:

- Extend ReachVectorScratch with values and child_flat buffer pools
- Replace per-node vec![] allocations in traverse_rv with pooled take/give
- Replace Vec<Vec<f32>> child_vals with flat contiguous buffer (n_actions * n_target)
- Refactor terminal_values_rv to write into pre-allocated buffer
- Persist ReachVectorScratch across iterations in the main solve loop
- Add PendingAccumulator::zero() for future cross-iteration reuse

Measured ~5.5% solve throughput improvement on 1x1 tiny fixture (11.9ms vs 12.6ms).
Larger gains expected on realistic spots with 50+ combos per side.

fix(nu): gracefully skip init when oh-my-posh is not installed (#7442)

* fix(nu): add early return guard when oh-my-posh is not available

If a user uninstalls oh-my-posh without removing the autoload script,
the script would still run on every Nu startup and produce errors.

Add an early return guard at the top of omp.nu that exits the script
immediately if the executable is no longer available. Handles both
install modes:
- Absolute path (standard install): \path exists\
- Base name only (MSIX / --strict mode): \which\

Fixes #7245

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(msi): accept WiX v7 EULA in build script

WiX v7 now requires explicit EULA acceptance under the Open Source
Maintenance Fee policy. Add -acceptEula wix7 to the wix build command
so CI releases don't fail.

See: https://docs.firegiant.com/wix/osmf/#direct-acceptance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(nu): guard path exists check to absolute/relative paths only

When the executable is stored as a bare name (e.g. MSIX / --strict mode),
\path exists\ resolves against the current working directory and may
incorrectly return true if an unrelated file with the same name exists
there. Only apply the \path exists\ check when the executable string
looks like a path (contains a separator or starts with . / ~); otherwise
rely solely on \which\ for the PATH-based lookup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

Cluster 32: 581→595 pass (+14), Cluster 29: 701→704 pass (+3)
